title: The Frogger

jokes.text: There are 200 people who work in this office. Every day is somebody's special day.
jokes.setup: Office cake celebration for Walter's special day
jokes.punchline: Mathematical reality that makes every day a forced celebration
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor that takes a relatable situation to its logical absurdist conclusion. Perfectly structured with economical delivery.

jokes.text: It's his birthday and it's his last day? / This is other Walter from returns.
jokes.setup: Confusion about celebrating birthday on last day
jokes.punchline: Reveals there are multiple Walters, explaining the confusion
jokes.explanation: Solid workplace confusion joke that sets up the office environment absurdity.

jokes.text: I had to take a sick day, I'm so sick of these people.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she's not at Walter's celebration
jokes.punchline: Literal 'sick day' for being sick of people - wordplay
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's perfectly in character for Elaine's workplace frustration.

jokes.text: Hey, speaking of tomato sauce, wanna come with me to Mario's Pizza?
jokes.setup: Jerry mentions spaghetti at 2am
jokes.punchline: George's non-sequitur connection to tomato sauce
jokes.explanation: Classic George non-sequitur that reveals his food-obsessed thought patterns.

jokes.text: Step back, son, there's nothing to see here.
jokes.setup: Kramer showing off police caution tape
jokes.punchline: Playing cop with the tape, complete with authority voice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer physical comedy - his childlike fascination with authority props.

jokes.text: You sure have a lot of friends. How come I never see any of them? / They wanna know why they never see you.
jokes.setup: Jerry questioning Kramer's mysterious friends
jokes.punchline: The mutual invisibility of their social circles
jokes.explanation: Nice back-and-forth that highlights the weird dynamics of Kramer's mysterious social network.

jokes.text: Oh, no, no, I had to fish around in the evidence room for it.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if police just gave him the tape
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of rifling through police evidence
jokes.explanation: Kramer's casual criminality delivered with complete innocence is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: It's just some guy, he's been running around Riverside Park, you know...cutting people's heads off.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking who 'the Lopper' is
jokes.punchline: Describing serial decapitation as 'no big deal'
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy through Kramer's casual delivery of horrific information. The understatement creates the humor.

jokes.text: What are the other titles? / Heads-o. The De-Nogginizer.
jokes.setup: Police having naming disputes about serial killer
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous serial killer name suggestions
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor that treats serious police work like marketing brainstorming. 'De-Nogginizer' is particularly inventive.

jokes.text: Son of Dad. / Yeah, that was my suggestion. It's sort of a catch-all.
jokes.setup: More serial killer name suggestions
jokes.punchline: Kramer's completely nonsensical 'Son of Dad' with proud ownership
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic from Kramer. 'Son of Dad' makes no sense but his pride in it as a 'catch-all' is perfectly Kramer-esque.

jokes.text: Remember us? / No. / We used to come in every day. / So where you been? We're tanking here.
jokes.setup: George trying to reconnect with Mario
jokes.punchline: Mario's brutal honesty about not remembering them and business failing
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing the harsh reality of nostalgic returns - nobody remembers you.

jokes.text: Oh, thanks. That'll save us. / All right, make it the large sodas.
jokes.setup: Ordering two slices and sodas at failing restaurant
jokes.punchline: Mario's sarcasm followed by Jerry's inadequate gesture
jokes.explanation: Nice beat about the futility of small gestures in the face of business failure.

jokes.text: Eat the fly. Eat it! / Thanks a lot. Beat it, punk.
jokes.setup: George intensely playing Frogger
jokes.punchline: Mario's hostile interruption of gaming nostalgia
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of George's Frogger obsession contrasted with Mario's hostility.

jokes.text: Perfect combination of Mountain Dew and mozzarella. Just the right amount of grease on the joystick.
jokes.setup: Explaining his legendary Frogger performance
jokes.punchline: Disgusting but specific conditions for gaming excellence
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - turning gross habits into a point of pride with scientific precision. The specificity makes it brilliant.

jokes.text: I think I remember why we stopped coming here.
jokes.setup: Mario's continued hostility
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dry acknowledgment of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry understatement that punctuates the scene perfectly.

jokes.text: So why do I keep smelling it?
jokes.setup: Pen smells really bad
jokes.punchline: The compulsive nature of smelling bad things
jokes.explanation: Relatable observational humor about inexplicable human behavior.

jokes.text: Get well, get well soon We wish you to get well... / Stop it! That's not even a song.
jokes.setup: Coworkers singing get-well song with cake
jokes.punchline: Elaine's frustrated interruption pointing out it's not a real song
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Elaine's breaking point with forced office cheer.

jokes.text: I mean, now we're celebrating a sick day?
jokes.setup: Get-well cake for someone who was out sick
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of celebrating illness
jokes.explanation: Strong observational comedy that escalates the office celebration theme logically.

jokes.text: Trying to fill the void in your life with flour and sugar and egg and vanilla? I mean, we are all unhappy. Do we have to be fat too?
jokes.setup: Attacking office cake culture
jokes.punchline: Reducing cake to basic depressing ingredients and existential despair
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that reduces cake to its depressing essence while delivering harsh existential truth. Classic Seinfeld cynicism.

jokes.text: Not you, Becky. I know you have a slow metabolism.
jokes.setup: Elaine's anti-cake rant
jokes.punchline: Awkward exception that makes everything worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment where trying to be considerate makes the insult worse.

jokes.text: End underwater. / Be that long. / So much more violent. / Not as long.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to have conversation with sentence-finisher
jokes.punchline: Every attempt derailed by wrong completions
jokes.explanation: Excellent sustained bit showing the frustration of conversing with someone who finishes sentences wrong.

jokes.text: Get going. / Eight tomorrow? / What you were thinking.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to politely end the date
jokes.punchline: Lisi finishing his thoughts to arrange another date
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the sentence-finishing bit that traps Jerry in unwanted plans.

jokes.text: Once you unplug the machine, all the scores will be erased.
jokes.setup: George's plan to buy the Frogger machine
jokes.punchline: The fatal technological flaw in his nostalgia plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect plot complication that creates the central conflict - technology vs. nostalgia.

jokes.text: Why must there always be a problem? You'd think just once, I could get a break. God knows I earned it with that score.
jokes.setup: Learning he can't move the Frogger machine
jokes.punchline: George's grandiose self-pity over a video game score
jokes.explanation: Pure George - treating trivial gaming achievement as life-defining accomplishment deserving cosmic reward.

jokes.text: There's a lot of people walking around that look like me. / Not as many as there used to be.
jokes.setup: Kramer saying murder victim looked like Jerry
jokes.punchline: Implication that the serial killer is targeting Jerry-types
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark humor delivered with perfect deadpan timing. The economy and dark implication make this exceptional.

jokes.text: I'm never gonna have a child. If I lose this Frogger high score...that's it for me.
jokes.setup: George's desperation about the Frogger situation
jokes.punchline: Equating video game score with having children as life legacy
jokes.explanation: Peak George absurdity - treating an arcade game as his only path to immortality. The character integration is perfect.

jokes.text: Slippery Pete? / I don't care for the name, either. In fact, that's one of the things that we argue about.
jokes.setup: Introduction of electrician named Slippery Pete
jokes.punchline: Kramer's casual mention of arguing about the nickname
jokes.explanation: Great character detail that reveals Kramer's contentious relationship while establishing the eccentric electrician.

jokes.text: She's a sentence finisher. It's like dating Mad Libs.
jokes.setup: Describing his dating situation with Lisi
jokes.punchline: Perfect analogy to children's word game
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy that perfectly captures the frustrating experience with maximum economy. The Mad Libs comparison is inspired.

jokes.text: Maybe I'll go raid Peterman's fridge. He's always got a truffle or something in there.
jokes.setup: Elaine needing her sugar fix after denouncing cake parties
jokes.punchline: Her boss having fancy food that contrasts with office cake
jokes.explanation: Sets up the cake subplot while showing class differences between Elaine and Peterman.

jokes.text: It's a cakewalk.
jokes.setup: Elaine finding Peterman's special cake
jokes.punchline: Literal cakewalk pun while committing cake theft
jokes.explanation: Serviceable pun that works in context but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Oh, what a stirring little anthem of wellness.
jokes.setup: Hearing the get-well song from his office
jokes.punchline: Peterman's pretentious mockery of office singing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Peterman pomposity - turning simple office singing into literary criticism.

jokes.text: Poor old Walt has a polyp in the duodenum. It's benign but, oh, still a bastard.
jokes.setup: Explaining Walt's medical situation
jokes.punchline: Calling a polyp a 'bastard' with dramatic flair
jokes.explanation: Classic Peterman - treating medical terminology with theatrical disdain. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: A slice of cake from the wedding of King Edward VIII to Wallis Simpson, circa 1937. The price? Twenty-nine thousand dollars.
jokes.setup: Peterman revealing his special acquisition
jokes.punchline: Paying $29,000 for 60-year-old cake
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Peterman purchase that sets up the main cake subplot. The specific price and historical detail make it brilliant.

jokes.text: She lives right off Riverside Park. I was scared of the Lopper, so I let her stay over.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he slept with Lisi
jokes.punchline: Fear of serial killer as excuse for sex
jokes.explanation: Good callback that connects the serial killer subplot to Jerry's relationship problems.

jokes.text: Guess what I ate? / An ostrich burger? / No. A $29,000 piece of cake.
jokes.setup: Elaine's mysterious eating revelation
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous value of what she consumed
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that escalates the cake subplot with the shocking monetary value.

jokes.text: It was the most romantic thing I've ever eaten. / How did it taste? / A little stale.
jokes.setup: Elaine describing the romantic royal wedding cake
jokes.punchline: Underwhelming taste after 60 years
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement - the contrast between romantic idealization and stale reality.

jokes.text: Well, they say ostrich has less fat, but you eat more of it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about expensive cake
jokes.punchline: Random ostrich meat observation
jokes.explanation: Typical Jerry random observation that doesn't quite land but fits his character.

jokes.text: Pennsylvania Dutch country? No, that's the serious-relationship weekend place.
jokes.setup: Learning about Lisi's weekend trip plans
jokes.punchline: Categorizing vacation destinations by relationship seriousness
jokes.explanation: Great Jerry observation about the relationship implications of different vacation spots.

jokes.text: I'm not going over there. That's where the Lopper is. / It's daylight. It won't take you that long. / Just make a clean break.
jokes.setup: Jerry afraid to go to Lisi's neighborhood
jokes.punchline: George's casual dismissal and unfortunate word choice
jokes.explanation: Good callback with dark irony in 'clean break' advice given the decapitation context.

jokes.text: No point in wasting 1200 bucks.
jokes.setup: George assembling his Frogger heist team
jokes.punchline: The cost of preserving a video game score
jokes.explanation: Shows George's twisted priorities - spending significant money to preserve gaming glory.

jokes.text: Oh, commander, isn't the wedding marvellous? The cake? Oh, I shouldn't. I mustn't. Ah, what the hell.
jokes.setup: Elaine fantasizing about the royal wedding while eating cake
jokes.punchline: Acting out being a prim guest who gives in to temptation
jokes.explanation: Fun physical comedy showing Elaine's imagination running wild while eating the historical cake.

jokes.text: Slippery Pete. Kramer tells me you are one hell of a rogue electrician.
jokes.setup: George addressing the electrician
jokes.punchline: 'Rogue electrician' as a character description
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment that makes electrical work sound like an adventure movie.

jokes.text: I don't know if I'm the best. / You're very good. / Let's say good. / Okay, good.
jokes.setup: George praising the truck driver
jokes.punchline: Negotiating down the compliment in real time
jokes.explanation: Awkward interaction that shows George's inability to give a simple compliment.

jokes.text: That was my mail-order bride. You weren't home, so I signed for her. / It doesn't give you the right to make out with her.
jokes.setup: Conflict between Kramer and Pete
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of mail-order bride and making out
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd backstory delivered casually - treating mail-order bride like a package delivery.

jokes.text: We're stealing this thing, right? / No, I paid for it. / I thought we were stealing it. / Feels like we're stealing it.
jokes.setup: Team discussing the legality of their mission
jokes.punchline: Everyone assumed it was theft and seems disappointed it's legal
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic where everyone preferred the illegal version of the plan.

jokes.text: Can I at least steal a battery? / Fine, steal the battery.
jokes.setup: Pete disappointed about not stealing
jokes.punchline: George allowing one theft to keep him happy
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the theft disappointment with George's accommodation.

jokes.text: That's where the electricity comes out of. / Oh, you mean the holes?
jokes.setup: George explaining 'outlet' to truck driver
jokes.punchline: Shlomo's simple description of electrical outlets
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from the language barrier and different technical vocabularies.

jokes.text: They put the Frogger with the toilet?
jokes.setup: Looking at Mario's Pizza layout diagram
jokes.punchline: Horror at video game placement near bathroom
jokes.explanation: Shows George's video game reverence being offended by unseemly placement.

jokes.text: Something pre-war would be just great.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking bakery for old cake to replace royal wedding cake
jokes.punchline: Requesting vintage baked goods like antiques
jokes.explanation: Good absurd request that treats cake age like wine vintage.

jokes.text: No, they're not really in the supermarket. They got their own case at the end of the aisle.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Entenmann's cake
jokes.punchline: Kramer's precise distinction about Entenmann's placement
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer attention to weird details - the geography of grocery store cake.

jokes.text: Well, I'm sorry if I ruined your life. That's exactly what I set out to do.
jokes.setup: Lisi accusing Jerry during their breakup
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic admission of malicious intent
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback that shows Jerry's frustration with the relationship accusations.

jokes.text: Boy, did your mother do a number on you.
jokes.setup: Extended breakup conversation
jokes.punchline: Jerry's psychological analysis delivered as insult
jokes.explanation: Standard breakup insult that escalates the relationship conflict.

jokes.text: We've been breaking up for 10 hours?
jokes.setup: Jerry realizing it's gotten dark during breakup
jokes.punchline: The marathon length of their breakup conversation
jokes.explanation: Great observational humor about the absurd duration of relationship drama.

jokes.text: Lopper. / We should give this a little more time. See how it looks in the light of day.
jokes.setup: Jerry hearing something outside and getting scared
jokes.punchline: Immediately reversing his breakup decision due to fear
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Jerry's cowardice overriding his relationship decision.

jokes.text: A long, long weekend. / I hear thee.
jokes.setup: Lisi planning their Pennsylvania Dutch trip
jokes.punchline: Jerry's faux-medieval response and Lisi finishing his thought
jokes.explanation: Decent continuation of the relationship dynamic with period-appropriate language.

jokes.text: Back to the wedding of one of the most dashing and romantic Nazi sympathisers of the entire British royal family.
jokes.setup: Peterman describing the historical cake
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Nazi sympathies in romantic context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that casually drops historical truth into romantic description. Perfect Peterman obliviousness.

jokes.text: I'd say about 219. / Two hundred and nineteen thousand dollars. / No. $2.19. It's an Entenmann's.
jokes.setup: Peterman expecting huge appraisal of royal wedding cake
jokes.punchline: The devastating revelation it's supermarket cake worth $2.19
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with devastating punchline. The structure builds expectation brilliantly before crushing it. Classic Seinfeld comeuppance.

jokes.text: Do they have a castle in Windsor? / No, they have a display case at the end of the aisle.
jokes.setup: Peterman's confusion about Entenmann's origin
jokes.punchline: Contrasting royal Windsor Castle with grocery store display
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that contrasts royal grandeur with mundane retail reality.

jokes.text: You idiots. You're gonna wear down the battery.
jokes.setup: Team members playing Frogger while George isn't looking
jokes.punchline: George's horror at them jeopardizing the mission
jokes.explanation: Shows George's obsessive control over his precious Frogger score.

jokes.text: I need holes. / The pharmacy's still open.
jokes.setup: George desperately needing electrical outlet
jokes.punchline: Kramer's solution using Shlomo's 'holes' terminology
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the electrical outlet language while showing desperation.

jokes.text: This reminds me of something. / I can do this. / By yourself?
jokes.setup: George realizing the street crossing is like Frogger game
jokes.punchline: George's confidence he can recreate his video game skills in real life
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup for the climactic visual gag where George's video game obsession meets reality. Perfect character integration - only George would think this way.

jokes.text: He looks like a frog. / So do you.
jokes.setup: George hopping across street with Frogger machine
jokes.punchline: Jerry's defensive comeback to the observation
jokes.explanation: Simple visual comedy with Jerry's characteristic defensive response.

jokes.text: Game over.
jokes.setup: George's attempt to cross street with Frogger machine
jokes.punchline: The machine announcing game over as George presumably fails/gets hit
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of the entire episode. The irony is devastating - George's obsession with preserving his Frogger score ends with 'Game Over' from the very machine he tried to save. Peak Seinfeld dark comedy and poetic justice.

jokes.text: But it also caught this.
jokes.setup: Peterman explaining his surveillance system caught Walter using his bathroom
jokes.punchline: Revealing he also caught Elaine eating his cake on camera
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Elaine's comeuppance through surveillance technology.

jokes.text: Do you know what happens to a butter-based frosting after six decades in a poorly-ventilated English basement?
jokes.setup: Peterman asking if cake is still with Elaine
jokes.punchline: Ominous implication about what 60-year-old cake does to digestive system
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy ending that suggests Elaine's punishment will be digestive horror. Peterman's scientific approach to revenge is brilliant.

jokes.text: I have a feeling what you are about to go through is punishment enough. Dismissed.
jokes.setup: Peterman's ominous question about old cake effects
jokes.punchline: Letting nature/biology handle the punishment instead of firing her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Peterman ending - theatrical mercy that's actually cruel. His confidence that biology will exact revenge is darkly brilliant.

tmdb_overview: George buys an arcade game to preserve his high score; fear of a serial killer adversely affects Jerry's social life; Elaine eats Peterman's slice of wedding cake from the nuptials of the Duke and Duchess of Windsor.
